proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027` equal to 2 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_027` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response for feature_027==2 vs feature_027==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 12.91% for `feature_027`=2 (960/7437) versus 23.10% for `feature_027`=0 (4063/17592); risk difference = -10.19 percentage points, two-proportion z-test p<1e-90.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-SD higher value of continuous `feature_004` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code: OLS linear probability model: objective_response ~ zscore(feature_004).
analyses.result_summary: A univariable linear probability model gave a -2.01 percentage-point change in `objective_response` per 1-SD increase in `feature_004` (z=-11.55, p<1e-30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-SD higher value of continuous `feature_031` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code: OLS linear probability model: objective_response ~ zscore(feature_031).
analyses.result_summary: A univariable linear probability model estimated -1.43 percentage points per 1-SD higher `feature_031` (z=-8.21, p=2.22e-16).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-SD higher value of continuous `feature_059` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code: OLS linear probability model: objective_response ~ zscore(feature_059).
analyses.result_summary: A univariable linear probability model estimated +0.90 percentage points per 1-SD higher `feature_059` (z=5.14, p=2.82e-7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-SD higher value of continuous `feature_033` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code: OLS linear probability model: objective_response ~ zscore(feature_033).
analyses.result_summary: A univariable linear probability model estimated -0.79 percentage points per 1-SD higher `feature_033` (z=-4.53, p=6.04e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each 1-SD higher value of continuous `feature_053` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code: OLS linear probability model: objective_response ~ zscore(feature_053).
analyses.result_summary: A univariable linear probability model estimated -0.65 percentage points per 1-SD higher `feature_053` (z=-3.74, p=1.82e-4).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with binary `feature_025` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_025` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_025==1 vs feature_025==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 23.46% for `feature_025`=1 versus 16.73% for `feature_025`=0; risk difference = +6.74 percentage points (z=16.88, p<1e-60).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with binary `feature_015` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_015` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_015==1 vs feature_015==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 22.10% for `feature_015`=1 versus 16.51% for `feature_015`=0; risk difference = +5.59 percentage points (z=15.40, p<1e-50).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with binary `feature_023` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_023==1 vs feature_023==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 21.96% for `feature_023`=1 versus 16.61% for `feature_023`=0; risk difference = +5.35 percentage points (z=14.74, p<1e-45).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with binary `feature_001` equal to 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_001` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_001==1 vs feature_001==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 16.81% for `feature_001`=1 versus 19.09% for `feature_001`=0; risk difference = -2.29 percentage points (z=-4.83, p=1.34e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with binary `feature_032` equal to 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_032` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_032==1 vs feature_032==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 16.57% for `feature_032`=1 versus 18.99% for `feature_032`=0; risk difference = -2.42 percentage points (z=-4.34, p=1.43e-5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_015` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_015` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_015, feature_025, and feature_015:feature_025.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015` by `feature_025` risk-difference interaction was +19.26 percentage points. Response was 34.95% when both were 1, versus 16.63% for `feature_015`=1/`feature_025`=0, 15.85% for `feature_015`=0/`feature_025`=1, and 16.79% for both 0 (p=2.11e-118).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_023` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_025, feature_023, and feature_025:feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` by `feature_023` risk-difference interaction was +19.08 percentage points. Response was 34.66% when both were 1, versus 15.96% for `feature_025`=1/`feature_023`=0, 16.49% for `feature_025`=0/`feature_023`=1, and 16.88% for both 0 (p=9.99e-117).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_015` with `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_023` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_015, feature_023, and feature_015:feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015` by `feature_023` risk-difference interaction was +15.28 percentage points. Response was 30.81% when both were 1, versus 16.30% for `feature_015`=1/`feature_023`=0, 16.05% for `feature_015`=0/`feature_023`=1, and 16.81% for both 0 (p=4.03e-91).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with all three binary features `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023` equal to 1 have an especially high `objective_response` rate beyond all lower-order pairwise combinations.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with all main, two-way, and three-way terms for feature_015, feature_025, feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: In the full 2x2x2 model for `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023`, the three-way interaction was +46.22 percentage points (p=9.61e-178). The all-three-positive subgroup had 63.31% response (1520/2401), while other cells ranged from 15.67% to 17.18%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_001` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_001` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_025, feature_001, and feature_025:feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` by `feature_001` risk-difference interaction was -9.58 percentage points. `feature_025` was associated with +8.19 percentage points when `feature_001`=0 but -1.39 percentage points when `feature_001`=1 (p approximately 1e-20).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_032` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_032` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_025, feature_032, and feature_025:feature_032.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` by `feature_032` interaction was -7.55 percentage points. Response was 24.24% for `feature_025`=1/`feature_032`=0 but 16.53% for both 1; p=6.71e-10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_015` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_001` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_001` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_015, feature_001, and feature_015:feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015` by `feature_001` risk-difference interaction was -5.95 percentage points. Response was 22.97% for `feature_015`=1/`feature_001`=0 but 17.12% when both were 1; p=8.90e-10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_023` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_032` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_032` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_023, feature_032, and feature_023:feature_032.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_023` by `feature_032` risk-difference interaction was -6.36 percentage points. Response was 22.60% for `feature_023`=1/`feature_032`=0 but 16.36% when both were 1; p=2.16e-8.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary `feature_074` equal to 1 is associated with a lower `objective_response` rate than `feature_074` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_074==1 vs feature_074==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 17.69% for `feature_074`=1 versus 18.85% for `feature_074`=0; risk difference = -1.15 percentage points. This did not reach p<0.05 (p=0.063).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary `feature_045` equal to 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_045` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_045==1 vs feature_045==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 18.92% for `feature_045`=1 versus 18.71% for `feature_045`=0; risk difference = +0.21 percentage points, not significant (p=0.627).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary `feature_040` equal to 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_040` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_040==1 vs feature_040==0.
analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` was 18.91% for `feature_040`=1 versus 18.62% for `feature_040`=0; risk difference = +0.29 percentage points, not significant (p=0.414).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `feature_019` with `objective_response` is more positive among patients in the top quartile of continuous `feature_047` (`feature_047` >= 128.05) than among patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_019, I(feature_047>=128.05), and their interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_019` by high-`feature_047` interaction was +3.23 percentage points. Response was 20.75% for `feature_019`=1/high-`feature_047` versus 17.24% for `feature_019`=1/lower-`feature_047`; p=0.00467.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `feature_023` with `objective_response` is stronger among patients in the top quartile of continuous `feature_031` (`feature_031` >= 73.3) than among patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_023, I(feature_031>=73.3), and their interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_023` by high-`feature_031` interaction was +2.18 percentage points. The `feature_023` response-rate difference was +4.80 percentage points below the threshold and +6.98 percentage points above it; p=0.00756.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of binary `feature_005` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients in the top quartile of continuous `feature_053` (`feature_053` >= 24.1) than among patients below that threshold.

analyses.code: Linear probability model with feature_005, I(feature_053>=24.1), and their interaction.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_005` by high-`feature_053` interaction was -4.78 percentage points. Response was 21.72% for `feature_005`=1/lower-`feature_053` but 15.65% for `feature_005`=1/high-`feature_053`; p=0.00904.
